northStar: Owner-operated software on Cloudflare adopts verified self-update by importing keel, deleting its own deploy-gate substrate.
constraint: Provider-agnostic library. No network unless a caller's port makes the call. Every claim has a runnable example and a receipt; projection never wears the costume of achievement.
falsifiable: Each example states a claim that could be wrong and runs a harness that would fail if it were.
proven: refuse-bad-self-update: a candidate that builds but fails a real live request is refused before promotion and rolled back to known-good (examples/refuse-bad-self-update, PASS).
stolen-token-useless: a leaked still-valid write token cannot promote a different candidate without a fresh signed proof bound to it; baseline unchanged (examples/stolen-token-useless, PASS).
one-key-cant-ship: under 2-of-3, a single stolen verifier key cannot reach quorum, and the transparency log detects an edited or removed authorization entry (examples/one-key-cant-ship, PASS).

audit-survives-restart: keyring revocations and a signed decision chain survive reload from disk, and an out-of-band store edit is detected by checksum / chain break (examples/audit-survives-restart, 7/7 PASS).

delete-the-handrolled-gate: a toy owner-operated worker deletes its hand-rolled gate by importing keel, ending 41 -> 25 owner LOC (39% fewer) while gaining signing, key windows, and compare-and-swap; two attacks the old gate accepted are now refused (examples/delete-the-handrolled-gate, PASS).
unproven: An out-of-tree, independent owner-operated worker deletes its own deploy-gate substrate against its real live deploy path. The leverage is realized within this repo's examples, not yet downstream.
openQuestions: Does the pre-promote live smoke generalize to a real workerd/wrangler preview runner injected by a Cloudflare consumer?
What is the smallest real downstream worker that would adopt keel and delete substrate, turning the leverage claim from within-repo to observed downstream?
